steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both texts must agree on the point. An agreement requires something explicitly stated in Text 1 AND Text 2 — not a detail from only one text, not an inference beyond what either says.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Compared with Salcedo's approach, the O'odham approach has the advantage of producing beans.: Text 1 does not compare the two approaches, and Text 2 makes no claim about the O’odham method having this advantage over Salcedo’s. Neither text asserts this. ✗
  • Choice B — Salcedo's team's method could be refined to more actively prevent soil moisture from evaporating.: Only Text 1 mentions soil moisture. Text 2 says nothing about it, so there is no shared agreement here. ✗
  • Choice C — Organ pipe cacti can provide shade that protects tepary beans from high-intensity heat and light.: Text 1 states organ pipe cacti provide shade that protects tepary beans from “the harshest heat and light.” Text 2 states tepary beans are planted “in the shade of sun-hardy species like organ pipe cacti for protection from the sun and heat.” Both texts make this claim. ✓
  • Choice D — Organ pipe cacti grow best when planted in shaded areas, while tepary beans do not require shade to thrive.: Neither text says organ pipe cacti need shade; Text 2 describes them as “sun-hardy.” This contradicts the texts. ✗
C is correct.
